key_insight: SoftVTBench shows that deformable-object manipulation must be judged by both task completion and physical safety, because many “successful” rollouts still slip, drop, or over-deform the object.
engineering_relevance: This benchmark gives robotics and solid-mechanics researchers a reproducible way to evaluate visuo-tactile policies under realistic deformation constraints, not just goal achievement.
method: Isaac Sim benchmark with finite-element-simulated deformable objects
Multi-modal inputs: multi-view RGB, RGB tactile marker motion, proprioception, and language instructions
Two metrics: Goal Success and Safety Success using hidden FEM states and object-specific deformation thresholds
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that success-only evaluation overstates performance, and that adding tactile sensing improves Safety Success from 21.4% to 35.6% on object-centric deformable tasks while keeping Goal Success comparable.
watch_item: Safety scoring depends on calibrated object-specific deformation thresholds and privileged hidden FEM states, so real-world transfer will require careful calibration and sensing equivalence.
paper_title: SoftVTBench: A Safety-Aware Visuo-Tactile Benchmark for Physically Constrained Robotic Manipulation of Deformable Objects
